skillName: Expand two single brackets and simplify
misconceptionSummary: Students expand each bracket but then combine unlike terms or leave the expression only partly simplified.

steps.narration: This answer is wrong because the constant term has been mixed into the x terms.
Only the x terms can be combined together. The 6 is a constant, so it stays separate.
Combine only the like terms. Two x plus x is three x, so the simplified expression is three x plus six.
The wrong answer treats the constant like an x term. The correct answer keeps different term types separate.

visuals.expression: After expansion, collect only like terms
visuals.label: Do not combine constants with variable terms

steps.narration: To avoid this mistake, sort the expanded expression into x terms, y terms and constants before simplifying.

misconceptionStrip.text: Common mistake: combining variable terms and constants into one term. After expansion, collect only terms of the same type.
misconceptionStrip.audioNarration: If the terms are not alike, they cannot be combined. Keep constants separate from variable terms.
